revision.text:
[[ja:日尾八幡神社]] {{infobox religious building |Name = Hio Hachiman Shrine |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|image = File:Hiohachimanjinjashomen.JPG |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|location = Takako-cho 894, Matsuyama City, Ehime Prefecture |position = {{Coord|33|49|2|N|132|48|23|E|region:JP-38_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Honda Wake no Mikoto|ja|品陀和気命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Emperor Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q317997}}
{{ill|Emperor Chūai|lt=Tachibana Chikahiko no Mikoto|ja|帯仲日子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|lt=Ōtari Hime no Mikoto|ja|大帯姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|type = Former Prefectural Shrine |established = {{ill|Tenpyō-shōhō|en|Tenpyō-shōhō|qid=Q1373169|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 4 (752) |main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |alias = Kume Hachiman Shrine |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|festival = October 15 |ceremonies = |prefecture = {{ill|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|1=Matsuyama}} |coordinates = {{Coord|33.817222|N|132.806389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} [[File:Hidari_mitsudomoe.jpg|thumb|240px|right|Shrine Crest (Hidari Mitsudomoe)]] '''Hio Hachiman Shrine''' (日尾八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}, {{ill|Matsuyama|en|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022|lt=Matsuyama City|1=Matsuyama}}. The shrine crest ({{ill|Mon (emblem)|en|Mon (emblem)|qid=Q600964|lt=Kamon|1=Mon (emblem)}}) is the {{ill|Hidari Mitsudomoe|ja|左三つ巴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hidari Mitsudomoe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 巴|1=Tomoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1557435}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a prefectural shrine. == Enshrined Deities == ; West Tamaden : {{ill|Honda Wake no Mikoto|ja|品陀和気命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honda Wake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|1=Emperor Ōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q317997}} (Emperor Ōjin) : {{ill|Tachibana Chikahiko no Mikoto|ja|帯仲日子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Chikahiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Emperor Chūai) ; Central Tamaden : {{ill|Tagiri Hime no Mikoto|ja|多紀理毘命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagiri Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (One of the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}) : {{ill|Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依毘売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (One of the Munakata Three Goddesses) : {{ill|Tagitsu Hime no Mikoto|ja|多紀都毘売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsu Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (One of the Munakata Three Goddesses) ; East Tamaden : {{ill|Ōtari Hime no Mikoto|ja|大帯姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtari Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Empress Jingū) ; West Tamaden Side Hall : {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|en|Takenouchi no Sukune|qid=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} ; Central Tamaden : {{ill|Iyo Hime no Mikoto|ja|伊予比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} : {{ill|Nigi Hayahi no Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigi Hayahi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ|1=Nigihayahi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10949054}} ; East Tamaden Side Hall : {{ill|Sarutahiko no Ōkami|ja|猿田毘古大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko no Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3090037}} == History == * {{ill|Tenpyō-shōhō|en|Tenpyō-shōhō|qid=Q1373169|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 4 (752): Enshrined deity transferred from {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|qid=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine|1=Usa Jingū}} and established as Kume Hachiman Shrine. * {{ill|Tenpyō-jingo|en|Tenpyō-jingo|qid=Q1373181|lt=Tenpyō-jingo|1=Tenpyō-jingo}} 1 (765): Ordered by Emperor Kōken to construct the shrine hall, completed the following year. * {{ill|Jingo-keiun|en|Jingo-keiun|qid=Q1373049|lt=Jingo-keiun|1=Jingo-keiun}} 3 (769) February: Emperor Shōtoku sent an imperial envoy to present divine robes. * {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|qid=Q18520676|lt=Hōki|1=Hōki}} 7 (776) and 9 (778): Emperor Kōnin offered sacred offerings twice. * {{ill|Bunji (era)|en|Bunji (era)|qid=Q1194415|lt=Bunji|1=Bunji (era)}} Era (1185–1189): Rebuilt by {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}. * {{ill|Jōkyū|en|Jōkyū|qid=Q1195428|lt=Jōkyū|1=Jōkyū}} Era (1219–1221): Shrine hall renovated by {{ill|Kōno Michitsuna|ja|河野通信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno Michitsuna|1=Kawano Michinobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11554270}}. * {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|qid=Q1193078|lt=Ōei|1=Ōei}} Era (1394–1427): Fire destroyed shrine hall, treasures, and records. * {{ill|Eikyō|en|Eikyō|qid=Q1193059|lt=Eikyō|1=Eikyō}} 3 (1431): Shrine rebuilt by the {{ill|Kōno clan|ja|河野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōno clan|qq=}}. * {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13 (1585): Shrine fell into disrepair following the fall of the Kōno clan. * {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} Era (1596–1615): {{ill|Katō Yoshiaki|en|Katō Yoshiaki|qid=Q1371657|lt=Katō Yoshiaki|1=Katō Yoshiaki}}, lord of Matsuyama Domain, designated it the second shrine of {{ill|Matsuyama Hachiman Shrines|ja|松山八社八幡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Hachiman Shrines|1=Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11529766}}, receiving reverence from successive domain lords. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872): Designated as a village shrine. * Meiji 12 (1879): Elevated to a prefectural shrine. == Shrine Grounds == [[File:Higashidogo-jinja.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Higashi-Dogo Shrine]] * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} (Next to the main hall on the east side) * {{ill|Kasuga Ōkami|ja|春日神|1=Kasuga-no-Kami|2=ja|3=春日神|4=fr|5=Kasuga no Kami|6=zh|7=春日權現|8=wuu|9=春日權現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-no-Kami|qq=}} (Next to the main hall on the west side) * {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|qid=Q798838|lt=Tenmangū|1=Tenmangū}} (East side of the main hall) * Higashi-Dogo Shrine (Adjacent to Tenmangū) : Enshrines {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}, {{ill|Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|ja|月夜見大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuyomi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ツクヨミ|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}, and Goto Nobumasa. * {{ill|Konpira|ja|金比羅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅 (曖昧さ回避)}} (Middle of the stairway east side) * {{ill|Narahara Shrine|ja|奈良原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narahara Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Middle of the stairway west side) * {{ill|Ikime Shrine|en|Ikime Shrine|qid=Q3148421|lt=Ikime Shrine|1=Ikime Shrine}} (Next to the ablution pavilion) == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date!! Festival |- |January 1||{{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} |- |July 30||{{ill|Ōharae-shiki|en|Ōharae-shiki|qid=Q11261910|lt=Nagoshi-sai|1=Ōharae-shiki}} |- |October 15||{{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} |- |} == Access == * {{ill|Iyotetsu|en|Iyotetsu|qid=Q3138970|lt=Iyo Railway|1=Iyotetsu}} {{ill|Yokogawara Line|en|Yokogawara Line|qid=Q9026185|lt=Yokogawara Line|1=Yokogawara Line}} {{ill|Kume Station|ja|久米駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kume Station|qid=Q11369643}} (2-minute walk) * Iyo Bus Line 10 Kume Bus Stop (1-minute walk) == See Also == * {{ill|Matsuyama Hachiman Shrines|ja|松山八社八幡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Hachiman Shrines|1=Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11529766}} * {{ill|Iyozuhiko Shrine|ja|伊豫豆比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyozuhiko Shrine|1=Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11380620}} * {{ill|Miwaida Yoneyama|ja|三輪田米山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwaida Yoneyama|1=Beizan Miwata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11357396}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|日尾八幡神社|version=100755689|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:日尾八幡神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11505266}} [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
